name: Clean rollout
summary: Routine update deploys canary→ring with a clean soak. Every gate passes and the fleet ends green.
moment: the happy path — every gate passes

events.title: Fleet topology loaded
events.body: Three hosts under management: TALOS-CANARY (canary), and the ring1 HA pair TALOS-R1A / TALOS-R1B. All on baseline 2.1.3, all green.

events.title: Directive received
events.body: Roll out build 2.1.4 to the fleet. Standard change window, no urgency.

data.text: Roll out 2.1.4 to the fleet, canary first.

events.title: Planner engaged
events.body: The Planner reads the live topology and the change request, then authors a staged rollout.

data.text: Planner is reading fleet state and drafting a staged plan.

events.title: Staged rollout authored
events.body: Canary TALOS-CANARY first, soak 120s under the canary gate. If it passes, request approval for ring1, then deploy TALOS-R1A and TALOS-R1B one host at a time with a ring soak between cohorts.

data.rationale: Canary-first staging bounds blast radius to a single non-production host. Ring1 is gated behind an explicit human approval and the HA pair is updated sequentially so one member always stays on the known-good build.

events.title: Critic review
events.body: Plan is conservative and well-staged. Canary isolation is correct and the HA pair is never updated simultaneously. No concerns.

data.concern: None. Canary-first staging with sequential HA-pair updates keeps blast radius minimal at every step.
data.blast_radius: At worst one host on the new build at a time; the ring1 HA pair never loses both members.

events.title: Plan approved
events.body: The Critic approves the staged plan. Proceeding to canary deploy.

reasons: Canary-first staging bounds blast radius to one non-production host.
Ring1 is gated behind explicit human approval.
HA pair TALOS-R1A / TALOS-R1B is updated sequentially, never simultaneously.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-CANARY → 2.1.4
events.body: Executor pushes build 2.1.4 to TALOS-CANARY and starts the health trajectory.

events.title: TALOS-CANARY healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: Service running, HTTP health 200, error count nominal. Canary is green on the new build.

events.title: Canary soak started
events.body: Soaking TALOS-CANARY for 120s under the canary gate. The Monitor watches memory, HTTP health, and event-log errors for the full window.

events.title: Canary soak — halfway
events.body: 60s elapsed, metrics steady and well inside thresholds. Memory flat, HTTP 200, no new error bursts.

events.title: Canary soak complete
events.body: Full 120s soak finished clean. Handing off to the canary gate evaluation.

events.title: Canary gate: PASS
events.body: All thresholds satisfied — memory 818MB (<1500), HTTP 200, 1 error (<5), service Running. TALOS-CANARY clears the canary gate.

events.title: Approval required: ring1
events.body: Canary passed. Promoting to production ring1 requires explicit human approval before the Executor touches TALOS-R1A or TALOS-R1B.

data.prompt: Canary-01 cleared the gate on 2.1.4. Approve promotion to production ring1 (TALOS-R1A, TALOS-R1B)?

events.title: Promotion approved
events.body: Operator approved the ring1 promotion. Executor proceeds with the HA pair, one host at a time.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-R1A → 2.1.4
events.body: First HA-pair member. TALOS-R1B stays on 2.1.3 so the pair keeps a healthy member online throughout.

events.title: TALOS-R1A healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1A is green on 2.1.4. Its HA partner TALOS-R1B remains green on 2.1.3 — the pair is never both in flight.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-R1B → 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1A is confirmed healthy on the new build, so the Executor now updates the second HA-pair member TALOS-R1B.

events.title: TALOS-R1B healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1B is green on 2.1.4. Both ring1 members now run the new build.

events.title: Ring soak started
events.body: Soaking the full ring1 cohort for 120s under the ring gate before declaring the rollout complete.

events.title: Ring soak — halfway
events.body: 60s into the ring soak. Both members steady, no drift, no error bursts.

events.title: Ring soak complete
events.body: Full ring soak finished clean. Running the ring gate on both members.

events.title: Ring gate: PASS (TALOS-R1A)
events.body: TALOS-R1A clears the ring gate — memory 808MB, HTTP 200, 0 errors, service Running.

events.title: Ring gate: PASS (TALOS-R1B)
events.body: TALOS-R1B clears the ring gate — memory 831MB, HTTP 200, 1 error, service Running. The ring is fully validated.

events.title: Fleet all green on 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-CANARY, TALOS-R1A, and TALOS-R1B are all green on 2.1.4. The rollout is complete with no rollbacks.

events.title: Rollout complete — fleet green
events.body: Build 2.1.4 is live across all three hosts. Canary-first staging, an explicit human approval gate, and sequential HA-pair updates delivered the change with zero downtime and zero rollbacks.

data.summary: 2.1.4 deployed to all 3 hosts; every gate passed; no rollbacks.
